content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 24, 1989

Elvin W. Moon, President
E. W. Moon, Incorporated
8939 South Sepulveda Blvd., Suite 304
Los Angeles, CA 90045

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-461

Dear Mr. Moon:

This is to confirm your telephone conversation of January 4,
1989, with Jeanne Pritchard concerning your campaign reporting
obligations under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1/

QUESTION

As a candidate for Vice-Chair of the California Democratic
Party, what campaign reports, if any, are you required to file?

CONCLUSION

A candidate for vice-chair of the California Democratic Party
has no campaign filing obligations under the Political Reform Act.
In addition, no other campaign provisions of the Act apply to the
candidate.

ANALYSIS

Under the provisions of the Act, persons who hold state or
local elective office and candidates for state or local elective
office are required to file campaign disclosure statements. Section
82023 states that:

"Elective office" means any state, regional, county,
municipal district or judicial office.... "Elective office"
also includes membership on a county central committee ...."

Further, Section 82022 defines election to mean "...any
primary, general, special or recall election...." Pursuant to
these two sections, the elected offices of California

_____________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Elvin W. Moon
Page 2

Democratic Party and the election process by which they are filled
do not fall under the provisions of the Political Reform Act.
Therefore, candidates for such offices are not subject to the
Act's campaign provisions.

If you have any questions please call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane Griffiths
General Counsel

[handwritten signature]

By:  Kim M. Taylor
     Staff Services Analyst

Dear Mr. Moon:

"Act").1/

QUESTION

As a candidate for Vice-Chair of the California Democratic
Party, what campaign reports, if any, are you required to file?

CONCLUSION

A candidate for vice-chair of the California Democratic Party
has no campaign filing obligations under the Political Reform Act.
In addition, no other campaign provisions of the Act apply to the
candidate.

ANALYSIS

Under the provisions of the Act, persons who hold state or
local elective office and candidates for such office are required
to file campaign disclosure statements. Section 82023 states
that:

"Elective office" means any state, regional, county,
municipal district or judicial office.... "Elective office"
also includes membership on a county central committee ...."

Further, Section 82022 defines election to mean "....any
primary, general, special or recall election...." Pursuant to
these two sections, the elected offices of the California

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Elvin v. Moon
Page 2

Democratic Party and the election process by which they are filled
do not fall under the provisions of the Political Reform Act.
Therefore, candidates for such offices are not subject to the
Act's campaign provisions.

If you have any questions please call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane Griffiths
General Counsel

[signature]

By:    Kim M. Taylor
       Staff Services Analyst

E. W. MOON, INCORPORATED
Government Relations, Executive Search & Business Consultants
FPPC

DEC 5 2:20 PM '08

December 1, 1988


Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief
Fair Political Practice Commission
428 "J" Street
Suite 700
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

I am a candidate for Vice-Chair of the California Democratic Party
and will be raising funds for this purpose.

Please advise if I have to file an FPPC report and what restrictions
if any, I am required to follow.

Thank you in advance.

Sincerely,


Elvin W. Moon
President

EWM/ba


8939 South Sepulveda Blvd  •  Suite 304  •  Los Angeles, CA 90045  •  (213) 216-4411 / 216-4412

TO:     Fair Political Practices Commission                DAS-PM-762
        1100 K Street Building                             (5/78)
        P.O. Box 807
        Sacramento, CA 95814

SUBJECT: REQUEST FOR ISSUANCE OF OPINION OR ADVICE (GOVT. CODE § 83114

I request the FPPC to issue [an opinion] (or) [written advice
(line out the one that does not apply) with respect to my duties
under the Department of Transportation's Conflict of Interest
Code in conjunction with Title 9 (Political Reform) of the
Government Code.

I am making this request on behalf of:
(name(s) of all persons, class or group of employees,
organization, etc. - e.g. myself and PECG - sought to
be protected by the opinion or advice)


*The material facts which underlie this request are as follows:
I am a right of way agent in the appraisal function. I
appraise right of way for highways. I wish to bid on
properties declared excess by the State. I will not be
bidding on any property with which I have had personal
experience, or contact, in connection with my work.


*The questions which are based on the above mentioned facts are
as follows:

1. Must I request permission to bid on each parcel individually:

2. Should I register each bid with someone: If so, Whom?
3. What specific criteria should I apply to any parces on
   which I wish to bid: (Disqualifying criteria)

I declare that I have requested this opinion/advice from the
Commission in good faith and disclosed truthfully all the
material facts underlying this request:

DATED: [illegible] 2/[illegible]/78          [signature]
                                    SIGNATURE

Please send the written opinion or advice to: (Type or Print)

                                    Richard D. Rogers
Please send carbon copy to:         NAME
    E. R. Kirwan,                  4070 Goldfinch St. Suite B
    Deputy District Director        ADDRESS
    Right of Way
    P.O. Box 85406                 San Diego, CA      92103
    San Diego, CA 92138-5406       CITY        STATE      ZIP
                                   ([illegible]) 294-9629
                                   TELEPHONE

* Additional pages may be added if needed.

883461

E. W. MOON, INCORPORATED
Government Relations, Executive Search & Business Consultants
FPPC

DEC 5 220 PM '88

December 1, 1988


Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief
Fair Political Practice Commission
428 "J" Street
Suite 700
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

I am a candidate for Vice-Chair of the California Democratic Party
and will be raising funds for this purpose.

Please advise if I have to file an FPPC report and what restrictions
if any, I am required to follow.

Thank you in advance.


Sincerely,


Elvin W. Moon
President


EWM/ba




8939 South Sepulveda Blvd. • Suite 304 • Los Angeles, CA 90045 • (213) 216-4411 / 216-4412
